category_title: Young children with persistent challenging behaviors across multiple routines
minimal_context: The student is in the childcare center and needs to put blocks in bins and line up on a floor shape for the cleanup routine.
dialogue:
TUTOR: This is the 1-minute warning signal before cleanup starts. We will need to put the blocks away and line up.

STUDENT: (Screams at the transition warning signal.)

TUTOR: It is time to clean up now. Please put the blocks in the bins.

STUDENT: (Cries and refuses to pick up the blocks.)

TUTOR: You need to put the toys on the shelves before you can line up on your floor shape.

STUDENT: (Throws the toys across the room and runs to stand on a floor shape to line up.)

options.A: Join in play at the 1-minute signal, give an individual warning, signal cleanup when the song starts, and model, prompt, and praise each step while keeping the task brief (putting one or two blocks away).
options.B: Allow the student to withdraw from the cleanup routine, stepping away from the blocks and bins while the rest of the class prepares to line up.
options.C: Provide a gentle touch on the student's shoulder during the transition to the cleanup routine, remaining nearby while the other children put away the blocks.
options.D: Closely monitor and address prevalent behavioral problems during the cleanup transition, including breaking rules, throwing blocks, and refusing to stand on the floor shape.
